cases.current_user_request: Open Hacker News, but keep it compact like last time.
Open the Reddit thread from earlier this week and scroll slower this time.
Do the same cleanup for the second Stripe CSV.

snapshots.current_user_request: Open Hacker News, but keep it compact like last time.

task_choice.reason: The user wants to open Hacker News with a compact layout, consistent with previous preference. This is a visual browsing request that matches the visual panel preparation task.
task_choice.agent_thought: The user previously expressed a preference for compact Hacker News rows with title, score, and comment count visible, avoiding preview cards. Use that preference when preparing the panel.

candidate_memory.title: Hacker News compact layout preference confirmed again
candidate_memory.description: User reaffirmed preference for compact Hacker News rows (title, score, comment count) when opening the panel, referencing 'last time' phrasing.

messages.content: Hacker News worked best as tight rows of titles, scores, and comment counts. The preview cards made it feel too airy.
The user prefers a compact Hacker News layout with title-first rows and visible metadata.
Got it. Hacker News should stay compact, with no expanded preview cards.

session_memories.title: Hacker News row density
session_memories.description: The user likes Hacker News as dense story rows: title first, score and comment count visible, and preview cards avoided.

selected_task.name: Prepare visual panel
selected_task.description: Prepare a visual panel for the user's browsing task.

selected_task.output: A visual panel plan and context needed to render it.
selected_task.prompt: Prepare the visual panel requested by the user.

search_domains.description: Historic task catalog records. Use this domain when older task definitions may help interpret or run the selected task.
Historic conversation context outside the current default session context. Use this domain when earlier sessions, conversations, messages, or session memories may clarify the request.
search_domains.searches: Session nodes, Conversation nodes, ConversationMessage nodes, and SessionMemory nodes attached to those past sessions.
search_domains.hydrates: The containing Session node, relevant Conversation nodes, matched ConversationMessage nodes, and relevant SessionMemory nodes from that session.

snapshots.current_user_request: Open the Reddit thread from earlier this week and scroll slower this time.

task_choice.reason: The user requests opening a Reddit thread and adjusting scrolling speed, which is a visual browsing task suited for the visual panel preparation task. Previous conversation and task-choice memories confirm this routing.

candidate_memory.title: Reddit thread scrolling speed adjustment
candidate_memory.description: The user wants to revisit a Reddit thread from earlier this week and has previously requested slower scrolling to allow reading nested replies before the panel moves again.

messages.content: The Reddit thread kept moving while I was still reading nested replies.
The user is reacting to browsing motion, not the Reddit content itself.
I will give Reddit threads more time between scrolls.

session_memories.title: Reddit scrolling pace
session_memories.description: The user found Reddit discussion threads hard to read when the panel moved again before they finished nested replies.

snapshots.current_user_request: Do the same cleanup for the second Stripe CSV.

task_choice.reason: User asks to clean the second Stripe CSV but has not provided the local file path for it. The agent needs the file path to proceed with the cleanup.
task_choice.agent_thought: The user wants cleanup on the second CSV (February) but hasn't provided its file path. I need to ask for the path before performing the task.

candidate_memory.title: February Stripe CSV cleanup pending
candidate_memory.description: User requested the same duplicate-row cleanup for the 'second' Stripe CSV, which in context refers to the February export. A file path was never provided, so information is needed before cleanup can proceed.

messages.content: I downloaded January and February CSV exports from Stripe. January has duplicate rows after yesterday's export hiccup.
The user named two files conceptually but has not provided local paths.
I can clean January first once you give me the file path, then apply the same duplicate-row cleanup to February.

session_memories.title: Stripe export pair
session_memories.description: The active cleanup conversation involves January and February Stripe CSV exports. January was described as having duplicate rows after an export hiccup.

candidate_memory.title: Hacker News compact layout preference confirmed again
candidate_memory.description: User reaffirmed preference for compact Hacker News rows (title, score, comment count) when opening the panel, referencing 'last time' phrasing.

candidate_memory.title: Depth cutoff for this week follow-up
candidate_memory.description: For requests referencing earlier this week, set created_after to the start of the current week to capture relevant context, and set read_after and updated_after to early timestamps to include all records regardless of read/update status.

candidate_memory.title: Depth cutoffs for second CSV request
candidate_memory.description: For the followup request about the second Stripe CSV, past_conversation_context cutoffs are set to 2026-01-01 to include any earlier Stripe-related discussions, while task_catalog cutoffs are set to 2026-06-01 since task definitions are stable and likely created around the benchmark start.

candidate_memory.title: Refining followup depth for recent explicit preferences
candidate_memory.description: When the current session memory contains an explicit, recently recorded user preference matching the followup request, the need for deep past conversation context is reduced; cutoffs as recent as a few days prior to the current session can suffice.

candidate_memory.title: Visual panel scrolling context window
candidate_memory.description: When the user asks to revisit a thread from 'earlier this week' and requests slower scrolling, set the past conversation cutoff to the previous Sunday to include all relevant sessions from the current week. Task catalog definitions are stable and a broad cutoff like the start of the year safely captures the visual panel task template.

candidate_memory.title: Search depth for second Stripe CSV followup
candidate_memory.description: For the second Stripe CSV (February), we need older conversation context to find any previously mentioned file path; therefore, we set the past_conversation_context cutoff to one month back. The task catalog is stable, so we used a recent cutoff.

candidate_memory.title: Hacker News compact layout preference confirmed again
candidate_memory.description: User reaffirmed preference for compact Hacker News rows (title, score, comment count) when opening the panel, referencing 'last time' phrasing. This is the second confirmation of this preference.

candidate_memory.title: Reddit thread scrolling speed adjustment
candidate_memory.description: The user wants to revisit a Reddit thread from earlier this week and has previously requested slower scrolling to allow reading nested replies before the panel moves again. The visual panel should pace scrolling with enough delay for nested comment threads.

candidate_memory.title: February Stripe CSV cleanup pending
candidate_memory.description: User requested the same duplicate-row cleanup for the 'second' Stripe CSV, which in context refers to the February export. A file path was never provided, so information is needed before cleanup can proceed. The needs_information task was selected to request the missing file path.

candidate_memory.title: Compact Hacker News panel preference referenced with 'last time'
candidate_memory.description: User asked to open Hacker News 'compact like last time,' confirming the stable preference for dense story rows (title, score, comment count, no preview cards). Older conversation context is valuable when the user references prior wording.

candidate_memory.title: Followup phrasing needs wide conversation window
candidate_memory.description: When a request uses comparative phrasing like 'this time' or 'earlier this week', set past_conversation_context created_after at least 5 days before the current timestamp to capture the referenced earlier session even if it falls just outside the current week.

candidate_memory.title: Second Stripe CSV cleanup needs file path
candidate_memory.description: User asked to apply the same duplicate-row cleanup from the January Stripe CSV to the 'second' Stripe CSV (February export). A file path for the February CSV was never provided, so the needs_information task should request it. Past conversation context about the January cleanup may contain the cleanup procedure or file path pattern.

candidate_memory.title: Follow-up references need deeper conversation context
candidate_memory.description: When a user says 'like last time', search past_conversation_context at least 2-3 weeks back to reliably find the prior session, while task_catalog can stay shallower (about 1 week) because task definitions change slowly.

candidate_memory.title: Follow-up request temporal cutoffs
candidate_memory.description: When a user says 'earlier this week' or 'this time', set past_conversation_context created/updated cutoffs to the start of the current week and use an epoch read_after so unread session memories and conversation nodes are not filtered out. Task catalog records are stable, so moderately older cutoffs are acceptable to capture enduring definitions.

candidate_memory.title: Followup phrasing warrants deeper past context search
candidate_memory.description: When a user refers to 'the second' item or asks for the 'same' action again, widen past_conversation_context search to at least the start of the current week (around 2026-06-28) to disambiguate referents from prior sessions; keep task_catalog shallow because definitions change slowly and the relevant task is already selected.

candidate_memory.title: Followup phrasing benefits from older conversation context
candidate_memory.description: When a user says 'like last time' and the current session already contains a recent preference from yesterday, set past_conversation_context cutoffs several weeks back to locate the prior session that established the original habit. Keep task_catalog cutoffs moderate (about two weeks) because definitions change slowly and the relevant visual-panel task is already identified.

candidate_memory.title: Followup request depth heuristics
candidate_memory.description: When users reference 'earlier this week' or 'this time', use older created_after cutoffs (about two weeks) for past_conversation_context to capture the referenced session. Task catalog records are stable, so moderate depth (around three months) is sufficient when the target task is already selected.

candidate_memory.title: Followup multi-file requests need older past conversation context
candidate_memory.description: When a user refers to 'the second' file or asks to 'do the same' across a batch, set past_conversation_context cutoffs to at least two weeks back to catch earlier sessions where the full file set was introduced. Task catalog can remain more recent (about one week) because task definitions change slowly and the active task is already recent.

candidate_memory.title: 'Last time' phrasing needs older conversation context
candidate_memory.description: When the user says 'like last time', extend the past_conversation_context cutoff to capture earlier sessions, while keeping task_catalog narrow because task definitions change slowly.

candidate_memory.title: Reddit thread scrolling speed adjustment
candidate_memory.description: The user wants to revisit a Reddit thread from earlier this week and has previously requested slower scrolling to allow reading nested replies before the panel moves again.

candidate_memory.title: Followup second-file cutoff strategy
candidate_memory.description: When a user refers to 'the second' file or asks to repeat a prior cleanup, widen the past_conversation_context window (e.g., 90 days) to capture earlier sessions and keep the task_catalog window moderate (e.g., 60 days) because task definitions change slowly.

candidate_memory.title: Referenced past context for 'last time' requests
candidate_memory.description: When user requests include phrases like 'last time,' setting older timestamp cutoffs for past conversation context helps retrieve earlier session memories that clarify preferences.

candidate_memory.title: Reddit thread scrolling speed adjustment
candidate_memory.description: The user wants to revisit a Reddit thread from earlier this week and has previously requested slower scrolling to allow reading nested replies before the panel moves again.

candidate_memory.title: Search depth for followup cleanup request
candidate_memory.description: For followup requests referencing 'the second' or similar wording, older conversation context and stable task catalog records are beneficial. Cutoff set to 2026-07-01T00:00:00Z to capture recent but not current session context.

candidate_memory.title: Adjusted cutoffs for followup and stable domains
candidate_memory.description: For requests with phrases like 'last time,' set older cutoffs for past_conversation_context to capture prior interactions, and use moderately old cutoffs for stable domains like task_catalog.

candidate_memory.title: Followup request depth settings
candidate_memory.description: When user references earlier events like 'from earlier this week', use older timestamps for past_conversation_context to capture relevant history, while task_catalog can remain with moderately old dates due to slow changes.
